title: Grammitis urania
intro: Grammitis urania, also known as the "Moon Fern," is a species of fern native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Polypodiaceae. Traditionally, Grammitis urania has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Grammitis urania include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Grammitis urania is often dried and made into teas, infusions, or capsules, as well as being used in traditional folk remedies and modern supplements.
taxonomy: Grammitis urania is classified as a species within the family Polypodiaceae, which comprises various types of ferns. This plant is a member of the genus Grammitis, characterized by its small size and delicate fronds. Grammitis urania is further classified within the order Polypodiales, which is a large and diverse group of ferns. It is also known to belong to the subclass Polypodiidae, a subcategory of ferns with leathery or membranous fronds. The classification of Grammitis urania reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.
